ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Set For Trial On Oct-07-19 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preference Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 36(A)
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of MAUNE RAICHLE HARTLEY FRENCH MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for plaintiffs and moving parties Giorgio Secchi and Cynthia Secchi.
Mary T. McKelvey and David K. Schultz of POLSINELLI PC (310-556-1801), appearing through Courtcall for defendant and opposing party Sequoia Ventures, Inc.

RULING:PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(A)
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is October 7, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 16, 2019. The Court denies the parties' request for a trial date of October 14, 2019 on the grounds that: 1) October 14, 2019 is a court observed holiday; and 2) the Court has an obligation to manage its trial calendar in light of the 120th day and the needs of the San Francisco Superior Court as a whole. (C.C.P. 128.) Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 3, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 20, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 4, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 18, 2019. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preference Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 36(A) (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than July 2, 2019, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later then July 2, 2019, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on August 21, 2019 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 23, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than September 6, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by September 27, 2019 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Wednesday, October 2, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court.

Parties stipulated on the record that defendant's motion for summary judgment or adjudication may file with 35 days notice. Opposition due 12 days prior to the hearing date. Reply is due 5 days before the hearing date. Parties to submit a stipulation for MSJ/MSA hearing notice. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kelly Roemer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 6435, kroemer@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 877-451-1580) (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preference Pursuant To Ccp 36(A) (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Trial Setting Preference Pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) is off calendar per the written request of the moving party. The Court's tentative ruling is vacated. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of MAUNE RAICHLE HARTLEY FRENCH MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for plaintiffs and moving parties Giorgio Secchi and Cynthia Secchi.
Mary T. McKelvey and David K. Schultz of POLSINELLI PC (310-556-1801), appearing through Courtcall for defendant and opposing party Sequoia Ventures, Inc.

RULING: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO CCP 36(a)

Hearing held, argued and Plaintiffs' motion for preference is continued by the court to Tuesday, May 21, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 503 for defendant to file an opposition. Opposition to be filed and served by May 13, 2019 by close of business. Plaintiffs' reply filed and served by May 16, 2019 by close of business. Courtesy copies required. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kelly Roemer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 6435, kroemer@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 877-451-1580) (503/CML)
